solution: Multiply by the conjugate: \( \frac{\sqrt{[[a2]]+x}-[[a]]}{x}\cdot\frac{\sqrt{[[a2]]+x}+[[a]]}{\sqrt{[[a2]]+x}+[[a]]} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{[[a2]]+x}+[[a]]} \). As \( x \to 0 \) this approaches \( \frac{1}{2\cdot[[a]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of 2 in the denominator \( 2\sqrt{a^{2}} \).
This has the wrong sign.
This inverts the correct value.
This mistakes the limit for \( a/2 \).
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: The denominator, not the numerator, has the radical here. Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to [[a2]]} \frac{x - [[a2]]}{\sqrt{x} - [[a]]} \).
solution: Rationalize the denominator by multiplying by \( \dfrac{\sqrt{x}+[[a]]}{\sqrt{x}+[[a]]} \): \( \dfrac{(x-[[a2]])(\sqrt{x}+[[a]])}{x - [[a2]]} = \sqrt{x} + [[a]] \). As \( x \to [[a2]] \) this approaches \( [[a]] + [[a]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to double \( \sqrt{x} \to a \) when the two terms combine.
This keeps only one copy of \( a \), dropping the \( \sqrt{x} \) contribution.
This doubles the correct value.
This mistakes the limit for \( a/2 \).
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\sqrt[3]{[[a]] + x} - [[k]]}{x} \) using the cube-root conjugate identity \( u - v = \dfrac{u^{3}-v^{3}}{u^{2}+uv+v^{2}} \).
solution: Let \( u = \sqrt[3]{[[a]]+x} \) and \( v = [[k]] \), so \( u^{3}-v^{3}=x \). Multiplying by \( \dfrac{u^{2}+uv+v^{2}}{u^{2}+uv+v^{2}} \) gives \( \dfrac{u-v}{x} = \dfrac{1}{u^{2}+uv+v^{2}} \to \dfrac{1}{3[[k]]^{2}} = [[answer]] \) as \( x \to 0 \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the exponent 2 on \( k \) entirely.
This forgets to square \( k \) in the denominator.
This forgets the factor of 3 from the three equal terms \( k^{2}+k^{2}+k^{2} \).
This inverts the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

solution: Combine the fractions over a common denominator and rationalize: \( \dfrac{1}{x}\left(\dfrac{[[k]]-\sqrt{[[a]]+x}}{[[k]]\sqrt{[[a]]+x}}\right) \); multiplying numerator and denominator by the conjugate \( [[k]]+\sqrt{[[a]]+x} \) and simplifying gives the limit \( -\dfrac{1}{2[[k]]^{3}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of 2 that comes from the two equal factors of \( k \) after cancelling.
This has the wrong sign, forgetting the order of subtraction.
This has the correct magnitude but drops the minus sign from the other direction.
This uses only one power of \( k \) instead of \( k^{3} \).
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
